name: La Sauceda, Aljibe Peak, and Laguna del Moral Loop
name_original: Carril Interior de la Sauceda – circular
description: Challenging 8.6-mile touring cycle loop through Los Alcornocales Natural Park, featuring Aljibe Peak views and the serene Laguna del Moral.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cortes De La Frontera navigate a landscape characterized by the mountainous terrain of the Serranía de Ronda, deep valleys like the Valle del Genal, and areas with significant elevation changes. The region features a mix of natural parks, including parts of Los Alcornocales, and offers routes that traverse both open country and forested sections. This area provides a challenging environment for touring cyclists, with numerous climbs and descents. Best touring cycling routes around Cortes De La Frontera The most popular touring cycling route is Serranía de Ronda - Valle del Genal – circular desde Jimera de Líbar, a 25.4 miles (40.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the mountainous landscapes and valleys of the region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Puerto de los Pinos Loop, a difficult 16.4 miles (26.4 km) path. This route features significant climbs and descents through a scenic, elevated area. Local touring cyclists also love the La Sauceda, Aljibe Peak, and Laguna del Moral Loop, a 8.6 miles (13.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain including a peak and a lagoon, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Cortes De La Frontera is defined by mountainous terrain, deep valleys, and natural park landscapes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Cortes De La Frontera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cortes De La Frontera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cortes De La Frontera?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes in the Cortes De La Frontera region, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The area is known for its mountainous terrain and challenging rides.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes in Cortes De La Frontera?
A: While the region is predominantly known for its challenging terrain, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. Most routes feature significant elevation changes, making them more suitable for experienced cyclists.

Q: What are the typical distances for touring cycling routes in this region?
A: Touring cycling routes around Cortes De La Frontera vary in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976864145/la-sauceda-aljibe-peak-and-laguna-del-moral-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sauceda, Aljibe Peak, and Laguna del Moral Loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 8.6 miles (13.9 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14569449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de la Pulga loop from Cortes de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt; extends to 39.4 miles (63.5 km).

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Cortes De La Frontera?
A: The terrain is characterized by the mountainous landscape of the Serranía de Ronda, deep valleys like the Valle del Genal, and significant elevation changes. You'll encounter numerous climbs and descents, traversing both open country and forested sections within natural parks such as Los Alcornocales.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see while cycling?
A: Along the routes, you can explore various natural monuments and mountain passes. Highlights include the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/857563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cat Cave&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/737774" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Barranca&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6491317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vulture Rock&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint. You might also cross historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5214944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Alemanes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974329720/serrania-de-ronda-valle-del-genal-circular-desde-jimera-de-libar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serranía de Ronda - Valle del Genal – circular desde Jimera de Líbar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a comprehensive tour of the mountainous landscapes and valleys.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Cortes De La Frontera?
A: The routes in Cortes De La Frontera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, rewarding descents, and the stunning natural beauty of the Serranía de Ronda and Valle del Genal.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Cortes De La Frontera?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler, wetter weather, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10297813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ronda Bullring – City of Ronda loop from Jimera de Líbar&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a demanding 32.4 miles (52.2 km) route with over 1190 meters of elevation gain, offering a significant challenge.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through mountain passes?
A: Yes, the area features several mountain passes that are often incorporated into touring cycling routes. Notable passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5226879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Encinas Borrachas&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5399251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Navazuelos&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2896293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Galiz&lt;/a&gt;, offering both scenic views and challenging ascents.

Q: What are the typical durations for touring cycling routes in Cortes De La Frontera?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976864145/la-sauceda-aljibe-peak-and-laguna-del-moral-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sauceda, Aljibe Peak, and Laguna del Moral Loop&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1 hour 30 minutes, while longer, difficult routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981488277/puerto-de-los-pinos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de los Pinos Loop&lt;/a&gt; can take over 2 hours 30 minutes.
tips: In the cemetery we find a monument erected in 2012 in honor of those who suffered reprisals in the El Marrufo massacre, where the largest mass grave of the Spanish Civil War is located in the province of Cádiz.
Excellent views of the Los Alcornocales and Pico del Montero Natural Park.

faqs.question: Is the La Sauceda, Aljibe Peak, and Laguna del Moral Loop suitable for beginner touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is considered moderate by komoot, but it presents a considerable challenge for touring cyclists, especially beginners. The trail features significant elevation changes with sections of up to 10% incline on forest tracks, and even steeper gradients (10-25%) in some parts, which may require dismounting and pushing your bike. It's best suited for experienced touring cyclists comfortable with varied and demanding terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from La Sauceda. You can find parking in the area around the village. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route in Los Alcornocales Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees to access Los Alcornocales Natural Park. However, certain activities or access to specific restricted areas might require permits. For cycling, it's always a good idea to check with the park's official visitor center or website for the latest regulations, especially if you plan to deviate from established paths.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this loop is during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural landscape is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making the strenuous climbs more challenging, while winters can bring rain and colder temperatures, potentially affecting trail conditions.

faqs.question: Is the La Sauceda, Aljibe Peak, and Laguna del Moral Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in Los Alcornocales Natural Park, they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Be mindful of the challenging terrain and potential for steep sections, which might be difficult for some dogs.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse terrain, including wide paths, forest tracks, and narrower, more rugged trails. You'll encounter significant elevation changes with steep ascents and descents. Sections, particularly towards Aljibe Peak and through gorges like Garganta del Moral, can be rocky and require careful navigation. Expect a mix of shaded areas under ancient trees and open sections with panoramic views.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in natural beauty and history. You'll pass through the historic village of La Sauceda, with remnants of its past. As you ascend, you'll reach Roble Viewpoint, offering stunning vistas. The summit of Aljibe Peak provides breathtaking 180-degree views. You'll also encounter the serene Laguna del Moral, a unique ecological spot, and picturesque gorges with small waterfalls.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: La Sauceda, the starting point, has some restored cabins for rural tourism. While it's a small, historic village, you might find limited options for cafes or restaurants directly at the trailhead. It's advisable to bring sufficient supplies for your ride and check for available services in nearby larger towns like Jimena de la Frontera or Ubrique before your trip.

faqs.question: Does this touring cycling route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several official trails within the park. You will cycle along parts of the Sendero La Laguna del Moral, the [CIMA CD03] Gáliz * Jimena de la Frontera, the La Sauceda trail, and the Sendero Travesía del Aljibe.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: Los Alcornocales Natural Park is a biodiversity hotspot. You might spot various bird species, including raptors, especially from higher elevations. The unique ecosystem of Laguna del Moral supports amphibians, reptiles, and insects. Mammals like deer, wild boar, and various smaller species also inhabit the cork oak and gall oak forests, though they are often shy and harder to spot.

faqs.question: Is there an insider tip for cycling this challenging loop?
faqs.answer: Given the significant elevation changes and technical sections, consider cycling the loop in a clockwise direction. While challenging either way, some cyclists find the ascents slightly more manageable and the descents less abrupt in this orientation, especially when navigating the rocky parts. Always ensure your bike is in excellent condition and carry appropriate repair tools.
